circular.title: Master Circular - Management of Advances - UCBs
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates instructions on management of advances by Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks, covering loan policies, working capital, credit administration, and specific lending activities. It outlines requirements for Board-approved loan policies, assessment methodologies, and credit administration protocols. The circular also provides guidelines on lending against gold, real estate, and to SHGs/JLGs.

clauses.text: UCBs must lay down transparent policies for credit dispensation with board approval, keeping in view credit exposure norms and RBI guidelines.
clauses.summary: UCBs must have transparent credit policies

clauses.text: Assessment for borrowers (other than MSE) up to ₹1 crore and MSE up to ₹5 crore can be made on projected annual turnover.
The loan component should normally be 80% for borrowers with limits of ₹10 crore and above.
UCBs must ensure interest rates are transparent, known to customers, and published in every branch.
UCBs must insist on a declaration that the account holder is not enjoying credit facilities with any other bank.
UCBs must ensure borrowers pay provident fund and other statutory dues promptly.
The Board must delegate specific powers to functionaries. Transgressions must be reported to the Head Office immediately.
UCBs are responsible for ensuring the proper end-use of funds.
UCBs must have a mechanism to monitor the end-use of funds.
UCBs must exchange information about the conduct of borrowers' accounts with other banks at least at quarterly intervals for limits of ₹5 crore and above.
Restructuring must preserve economic value and not be used for evergreening.
The grant of bridge loans or interim finance to any company is totally prohibited.
UCBs must frame comprehensive prudential norms relating to ceilings on total real estate loans and exposure limits.
clauses.summary: Prudential norms for real estate loans

clauses.text: Loans against gold ornaments must not exceed ₹2 lakh (increased to ₹4 lakh for UCBs meeting specific PSL targets).
UCBs must only purchase/discount bills under LCs for genuine commercial transactions of their constituent borrowers.
Lending must be direct; intermediaries are not permitted.
UCBs must evolve a policy framework for providing relief in areas officially declared as affected by calamities.

obligations.text: Formulate transparent Board-approved policies for credit dispensation for each economic activity.
obligations.plain_english: Create clear credit policies approved by the Board

obligations.text: Review the loan policy at least once in a financial year to align with risk appetite and regulations.
Maintain a 80:20 Loan/Cash Credit mix for borrowers with working capital limits ≥ ₹10 crore.
obligations.plain_english: Maintain 80:20 loan to cash credit ratio

obligations.text: Provide a Key Fact Statement (KFS) to all borrowers for loans and advances.
obligations.plain_english: Provide Key Fact Statement to borrowers

obligations.text: Obtain a 'No Objection Certificate' (NOC) from existing financing banks before extending new credit.
obligations.plain_english: Obtain NOC from existing financing banks

obligations.text: Ensure penal charges for non-compliance are not capitalized (no interest on charges).
Prohibit the grant of bridge loans or interim finance to any company.
Maintain a Loan to Value (LTV) ratio not exceeding 75% for gold jewellery loans.
obligations.plain_english: Maintain LTV ratio for gold loans

obligations.text: Limit total loan repayment obligations of a microfinance household to 50% of monthly income.
File security interests relating to immovable, movable, and intangible assets with CERSAI.

prohibitions.text: The grant of bridge loans or interim finance to any company is totally prohibited.
Advances for the purchase of gold in any form (bullion, coins, etc.) are prohibited.
prohibitions.plain_english: Do not advance for gold purchase

penalties.condition: Failure to maintain LTV ratio for gold loans

definitions.definition: The sum of demand and time deposits.
Liabilities payable on demand, including current deposits, demand portions of savings deposits, margins against LC/guarantees, and unclaimed deposits.
Liabilities payable otherwise than on demand, including fixed deposits, cash certificates, and gold deposits.
An exposure where the realizable value of the security is not more than 10%, ab-initio, of the outstanding exposure.
Farmers with land holdings of 5 acres or less.
A collateral-free loan to a household having annual income up to ₹3,00,000.

references.circular_number: Master Direction – RBI (Credit Information Reporting) Directions, 2025
Master Direction on Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters (July 30, 2024)
Master Directions - RBI (Priority Sector Lending – Targets and Classification) Directions, 2025
Master Direction - Know Your Customer (KYC) Direction, 2016
